claim.statement: Confirm bulk actions that affect many objects at once, showing the exact count and scope before executing (e.g. 'Delete 142 records'). Bulk operations multiply the cost of a mistake, so the confirmation should state how many items are affected and whether the change is reversible.

limitations: Frequent low-risk bulk edits may warrant undo instead of a blocking confirmation

do: Show the exact affected count and scope in the confirmation
Distinguish 'selected' vs 'all matching filter' selections explicitly
Offer undo for reversible bulk operations
avoid: Executing a bulk delete/update with no count or scope shown
Ambiguous 'select all' that silently spans beyond the visible page

strategies: add undo for reversible bulk ops
